description: the system we use for placing child assets onto anchor points is messed up and different between normal game mode and dev mode. the actual anchor points in both modes are rendered propperly in their correct positions (as per visuall inspection with anchor point debuging on) but the child placement of assets onto anchor points is incosistant between dev mode where it is correct placement and in normal game mode where they are placed incorrectly. in normal game mode its like the math for placement is fucked up. as the player moves more left the assets will move more  right and vice versa like some sort of placment inversion. or in normal dev mde thechild assets wont show up at all (even if they have valid points) in dev mode the child assets render perfectly. make sure the normal game mode and dev mode have the exact same child asset updating/placment system and are accuratly and consistantly placed between the 2 modes always. implement this cleanly

the dev mode boundary asset config pannel and map wide assets pannel are both functional, but the ui and usage of the 2 look different. the options and usage and look for both pannels should be functionally identical they are just controlling info related to 2 different types of spwn group things. make the map wide assetspannel look and work and hve the same exact usage and options as the boundary assets pannel. dont chnage the boundary assets pannel as that one looks and works perfectly i just want map wide assets pannel to look and feel and function (ui wayat leaast) the exact same way) they still controll the exact same functions they do currently this is just a pure panel asthetic and utility chnage for map wide assets pannel

the render area is incorect. as you can see from the photo the render area does not extend far enough down. things etend outward enough but its like things dontrender below the middle of the screen. investigate and fix.

Clean up the animation editor workflow. Remove the geometry and animation dropdowns since they are unused. Remove the anchors button from the geometry dropdown. Remove the configure animations button from the asset info UI. The animation editor should open via tab navigation and appear immediately after the asset info panel. When opened, it must be full screen with no docked asset info panel visible. Do not display the frame or animation navigator while the animation editor is open.

Attack hitbox system is completely broken. It does not render in the editor, is not saved on close, and does not exist at runtime after being added. Assume both hitbox and attack box systems are affected. Codex must analyze the attached screenshot to understand the issue, then trace the full pipeline including creation, rendering, serialization, and runtime loading. Identify and fix the root cause so hitboxes function correctly in both editor and runtime.
